old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-865m","title":"app_identity: upsert_peer copies input then re-sanitizes display_name","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_identity/app_identity.c:224-228

After the TOFU check, the code:
1. memset(&s_peers[slot].peer, 0, ...) - zeros everything (redundant, separate bead)
2. s_peers[slot].peer = *peer - full struct copy including display_name
3. copy_string(s_peers[slot].peer.display_name, ..., peer->display_name) - re-copies display_name

Step 3 is sanitizing the display_name to ensure zero-padding after the NUL terminator. However, the struct copy in step 2 already copied display_name with its original content (which passed has_nul validation).

The sanitization makes sense for security (ensuring no information leakage in unused buffer bytes), but the struct copy followed by copy_string is doing double work on display_name.

More efficient approach:
  s_peers[slot].peer = *peer;
  memset(s_peers[slot].peer.display_name + strlen(peer->display_name) + 1, 0,
         sizeof(s_peers[slot].peer.display_name) - strlen(peer->display_name) - 1);

Or selectively copy fields without display_name then use copy_string once.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"cleanup","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:40:34.982009Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:40:34.98204Z"}

new_value: Bugs fixed in codec.rs fragment.rs app_identity.c; added mixed/ULA support, consistency verification, out-of-window rejection, cleaned redundant ops; no comments/dead code; tests/lint pass
